content.problemStatement:
Chef's last task in his internship requires him to construct 222 permutations, each having length NNN, such that the bitwise AND of each pair of elements at every index is the same.

Formally, if the permutations are AAA and BBB, then, the value Ai&BiA_i \& B_iAi​&Bi​ must be **constant** over all 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N.

Help Chef by providing him 222 such permutations. If it is not possible to construct such permutations, print −1-1−1 instead.

A permutation is a sequence of integers from 111 to NNN of length NNN containing each number exactly once.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, a single integer NNN denoting the size of the required permutations.


Output Format

If there are valid permutations that satisfy the required condition, then:

In the first line, output NNN space-separated integers denoting the permutation AAA.
In the second line, output NNN space-separated integers denoting the permutation BBB.


If there is no valid pair of permutations, print `-1` in a single line.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one element in each array and the resultant bitwise AND is 111. Thus, A=[1]A = [1]A=[1] and B=[1]B = [1]B=[1].
Test case 222: Let A=[1,2]A = [1,2]A=[1,2] and B=[2,1]B = [2,1]B=[2,1]. The resultant bitwise AND of each pair at any index is equal to 000. So, this is a valid pair of permutations.
Test case 333: There are 666 permutations of length 333. It can be seen that any pair of the 666 permutations does not satisfy the stated condition.
